characters_found.name: Hadji Murad
direct_quotes: My murid.  Eldar is his name
speech_descriptors: asked Hadji Murad, addressing the old man
actions: takes off his burka, rifle, and sword and hands them to the old man
adjusts the pistol at his back
comes up to the cushions
wraps his Circassian coat closer round him and sits down
mirrors the old man's prayer posture — closes eyes, lifts hands palms upwards
repeats a prayer and strokes his face, palms joining at the end of his beard
understands the old man does not wish to say what he knows
slightly nods his head and asks no more questions
introduces his murid Eldar to the old man
inner_states: understands (tacitly reads) that the old man is withholding information he wants to know
exercises restraint — chooses not to press further after picking up the signal
social_markers: Caucasian Muslim warrior — carries burka, rifle, sword, and concealed pistol
ritually observant — performs the shared prayer gesture with the old man
travels with a murid (disciple/bodyguard), marking him as a figure of rank

relationship_dynamics.quality: respectful, ritually synchronized, patient; reads the old man's reticence and defers without confrontation
leader to disciple — introduces and vouches for him; Eldar mirrors his disarming ritual exactly

new_info: carries a pistol concealed at his back in addition to the weapons he surrenders
observes a shared Muslim prayer ritual with his host before conversing
speaks the local greeting formula "Ne habar?"
reads subtle social cues and withdraws questions rather than pushing for information

characters_found.name: Sado's Father (Old Man)
direct_quotes: I live at the apiary and have only today come to see my son....He knows.

The only news is that the hares keep discussing how to drive away the eagles, and the eagles tear first one and then another of them. The other day the Russian dogs burnt the hay in the Mitchit aoul....May their faces be torn!
speech_descriptors: replied the old man, looking with his lifeless red eyes not at Hadji Murad's face but at his breast
asked the old man, pointing to the newcomer
said the old man, and motioned Eldar to a place on a piece of felt beside Hadji Murad
actions: carefully hangs Hadji Murad's rifle and sword on a nail beside the master of the house's weapons
squats on his bare heels beside Hadji Murad
closes his eyes and lifts his hands palms upwards in prayer
strokes his face, palms joining at the end of his beard
avoids eye contact — looks at Hadji Murad's breast rather than his face
points to Eldar when asking who he is
motions Eldar to a place on a piece of felt beside Hadji Murad
inner_states: withholds something he knows but does not wish to say
bitter, angry grief over Russian depredations — curses "May their faces be torn!"
resigned fatalism expressed through the hares-and-eagles parable
social_markers: elderly Caucasian Muslim villager — goes barefoot, squats on heels
refers to Russians pejoratively as "Russian dogs"
hoarse, angry register when speaking of Russian atrocities

relationship_dynamics.quality: deferential yet guarded — performs the host's ritual duties (hanging weapons, shared prayer) but avoids eye contact and withholds information
father of the house's master; defers knowledge of current affairs to his son ("He knows")
cautiously accepting — questions his identity, then approves ("That is well") and assigns him a place
hostile, grieving, cursing — treats them as destructive predators

new_info: is the father of Sado (the master of the house)
lives at an apiary and only came today to visit his son
has "lifeless red eyes" — physical detail of age/fatigue
speaks in proverbs (hares/eagles) to describe the Caucasian–Russian conflict
reports a recent Russian atrocity: burning of hay in the Mitchit aoul
performs the host's ritual of hanging a guest's weapons
defers current news to his son, signalling limited personal knowledge of Hadji Murad's situation

characters_found.name: Eldar
actions: enters the room, his strong legs striding softly over the earthen floor
retains only his dagger and pistol
takes off his burka, rifle, and sword, mirroring Hadji Murad's disarming
hangs his weapons on the same nails as his leader's
sits down on the piece of felt indicated by the old man, crossing his legs
social_markers: Caucasian Muslim warrior / murid — armed with dagger, pistol, rifle, sword, burka
moves quietly despite his strength ("striding softly")
junior rank — mirrors his leader's actions rather than initiating
accepts a lesser seat (piece of felt beside Hadji Murad, not cushions)

relationship_dynamics.quality: murid/disciple — follows and mirrors him exactly, defers in seating and ritual
silent subordinate — is questioned about, not addressed; obeys the old man's motion to sit

new_info: has physically strong legs but moves softly — a quiet, capable warrior
carries dagger, pistol, rifle, sword, and burka
seated on felt (lower status) next to Hadji Murad on the cushions
